title: LitT encoder determinism — pin the literal-table bytes for cross-consumer identity
purpose: The `LitT` encoder compresses the literal-table body with `Compression::default()` over a caret-versioned `flate2 = "1.1.9"` (`crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/literals.rs:43`; `crates/beamr/Cargo.toml:26`, both at `d60f826`). Compressed output is not a stable function of input: it varies with the flate2/miniz_oxide version and backend that the FINAL build graph resolves, so identical literal tables can encode to different bytes on different builds with zero code changes on beamr's side. CLAIM SCOPE, ruled by the claim-holder (Vesper Lynd via the board, 2026-07-15, Pole-B-critical): CROSS-CONSUMER determinism — aion hashes emitted BEAM bytes into durable package version identity, so identical literal tables must encode identically for downstreams resolving their own dependency graphs. A lockfile-plus-golden-fixture defence is insufficient for that claim: the fixture runs in beamr's CI, not the consumer's build, so a downstream with a different miniz_oxide drifts silently past the wall. The decode side is indifferent to the fix: `decode_literal_chunk` accepts a zero size-prefix as an UNCOMPRESSED table read directly, and otherwise inflates bounded (`crates/beamr/src/loader/decode/chunks.rs:307-313`; bomb guard `crates/beamr/src/loader/decode/chunks.rs:350-374`). MEASURED GROUND at `d60f826`, strengthened tree-wide at the fold (reviewer's pen fix, independently re-verified): 36 git-tracked `.beam` files carry a `LitT` chunk — 22 under `crates/beamr/tests/fixtures/`, 14 under `test-workflows/` — and every single one uses the zero-prefix UNCOMPRESSED form; ZERO compressed `LitT` chunks exist anywhere in the tracked tree. The erlc/gleam toolchain that produced the committed corpus does not compress literal tables. Re-deflating the 22 `crates/beamr` fixture tables at level 6 saves 2,448 bytes total across that fixtures corpus (43% of 5,657 raw table bytes; the largest single table is 1,830 bytes raw → 746 deflated). Compression on these tables buys kilobytes per corpus, not per module.
task: Make `encode_literal_chunk` emit bytes that are a pure function of the literal table — no ambient compressor default, no dependency-resolved variance — and wall the result with a committed golden fixture. `Compression::default()` dies in this file. THREE CANDIDATES, decision at the tear: (A) EXACT-PIN the backend — `=`-pin flate2 and add a direct `=`-pinned miniz_oxide dependency so every consumer graph unifies on those exact bytes. Delivers the claim but exports resolution rigidity onto every downstream sharing miniz_oxide, and re-fragilizes at every flate2 minimum-version bump; the golden fixture then guards a dependency's mood, not a construction. (B) DETERMINISM BY CONSTRUCTION, stored-block zlib — beamr emits its own canonical stored-block zlib stream (fixed segmentation at the 65,535-byte block bound, adler32 trailer; a small emitter producing valid zlib for any inflater). Bytes independent of any compressor's choices forever; measured cost ~11 bytes of framing per table versus the raw form, i.e. the full deflate saving (43% of table bytes on the corpus) is forgone. The golden fixture then guards construction. (C) RECOMMENDED — emit the UNCOMPRESSED zero-prefix form the decoder already accepts and the committed toolchain corpus already uses: size prefix 0, raw table bytes. Determinism by construction with NO compressor and NO new emitter — the change is a deletion; flate2 leaves the encode path entirely (decode keeps it for legacy compressed chunks); beamr-emitted modules become byte-symmetric with what erlc/gleam themselves emit in the committed corpus (all 22 LitT fixtures, verifiable with `docs/design/beamr/briefs/`-independent parsing of `crates/beamr/tests/fixtures/**.beam`). Size cost identical to B minus the zlib framing. RESIDUAL RISK, named: a foreign (non-beamr) consumer of beamr-EMITTED `.beam` files whose loader predates zero-prefix support would fail to load them; beamr's own decoder accepts the form at the cited bytes and the current toolchain emits it, but the brief makes no claim about older third-party loaders. TEAR RULINGS (torn APPROVED, reviewer-of-record, 2026-07-14 — the former open questions are DECIDED): (1) CANDIDATE C CONFIRMED — determinism by deletion beats determinism by construction beats determinism by pinning: the decoder accepts the form at the cited bytes, the entire committed corpus already speaks it, and byte-symmetry with the toolchain's own emission is worth more than the corpus-wide deflate savings. (2) FOREIGN-LOADER COMPATIBILITY IS A NON-CLAIM: beamr-emitted `.beam` files claim loadability by beamr's own decoder and byte-symmetry with the modern erlc/gleam corpus — nothing more. No cited consumer loads beamr-EMITTED modules through a third-party loader (aion hashes bytes; it does not load them through OTP tooling), and the toolchain corpus proves modern emitters use the form. Candidate B is the NAMED FALLBACK SHAPE if a real foreign-loader requirement ever arrives with a citation attached; a requirement nobody can cite does not kill the clean candidate. (3) GOLDEN-FIXTURE PLACEMENT: in-test expected byte array, NOT a committed binary — Vesper's mandatory review runs over the final diff, and a hex array is reviewable in that diff where a binary blob is not; the test carries a comment stating the regeneration procedure so a legitimate future change re-derives rather than hand-edits. Any need to change the decode side, any new configuration surface, or any silent fallback to an ambient compressor default remains STOP-and-report.

requirements.title: Replace the ambient-default compression call with the tear-ruled deterministic form
requirements.spec: Rework the tail of `encode_literal_chunk` (`crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/literals.rs:42-54` at `d60f826`) per TEAR-RULED CANDIDATE C: write a zero u32 size prefix followed by the raw payload — the exact form `decode_literal_chunk` reads at `crates/beamr/src/loader/decode/chunks.rs:308-310` and the form every one of the 36 tracked `LitT`-carrying `.beam` files already uses. Remove the `flate2` imports from this file (`crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/literals.rs:13-14`) and retire `EncodeError::CompressionFailed` if nothing else raises it (`crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/container.rs:34-45`); flate2 leaves the encode path entirely and remains a dependency only for decode. The encoder consults no ambient default, no environment, and no configuration surface; the emitted bytes are a pure function of the literal table. The module documentation comment (`crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/literals.rs:1-11`) is updated in the same commit to describe the zero-prefix form — a stale doc claiming zlib compression over a zero-prefix emitter is a truthfulness defect. Candidates B (canonical stored-block zlib emitter) and A (exact-pinned backend) remain recorded in the task field as decision ground; B is the named fallback shape per Ruling 2 and is NOT built here.
acceptance: Encoding the same literal table twice yields byte-identical output, and `Compression::default()` appears nowhere under `crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/`

A module encoded by beamr carries a `LitT` chunk whose first four bytes are zero and whose remainder is the raw table, `decode_literal_chunk` round-trips it, and no `flate2` import remains under `crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/`
The existing loader round-trip and encode tests remain green unmodified except where they pin the old compressed form
The module doc comment describes the ruled form accurately

requirements.title: Wall the bytes with a committed golden fixture covering every Literal variant
requirements.spec: Add a golden-bytes wall: encode one representative literal table exercising every `Literal` variant and every width boundary the encoder branches on — `SMALL_INTEGER_EXT`/`INTEGER_EXT`/`SMALL_BIG_EXT` integer widths (`crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/literals.rs:125-143`), float, untrimmed big-integer magnitude (`crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/literals.rs:149-166`), small and large atom names, binary, small and large tuple, nil, list with non-nil tail, map, string, and export fun — and assert byte-exact equality against golden bytes carried as an IN-TEST EXPECTED BYTE ARRAY (TEAR-RULED, Ruling 3: not a committed binary — Vesper's mandatory review runs over the final diff and a hex array is reviewable there; the test carries a comment stating the regeneration procedure so a legitimate future change re-derives rather than hand-edits). Assert the golden bytes also DECODE back to the identical literal table through `decode_literal_chunk`, so the wall pins the round trip, not just the emitter. Proposed names `litt_encode_matches_golden_bytes` and `litt_golden_bytes_round_trip` (the tear may rename; any rename updates this brief's mapping in the same commit). This wall is what fails loudly if any future change — emitter or refactor — moves the emitted bytes: it guards construction.
acceptance: `litt_encode_matches_golden_bytes` asserts byte-exact equality between a fresh encode of the representative table and the committed golden bytes, and covers every `Literal` variant and integer-width branch
`litt_golden_bytes_round_trip` decodes the committed golden bytes and asserts structural equality with the source table
Fail-first, recorded and reverted: perturb one emitted byte and show the golden wall fails
Both walls pass ten consecutive isolated runs

requirements.title: State the identity shift out loud — changed bytes move downstream version hashes
requirements.spec: The claim-holder's reviewer note, carried as a requirement so it is stated rather than discovered (Vesper Lynd, 2026-07-15): pinning changes the encoded bytes versus today's default-deflate output, which shifts aion package version hashes on the next recompile of any direct-BEAM package — EXPECTED AND CORRECT (version identity moves when bytes move), and never to be read as a regression. Add a CHANGELOG entry under the next unreleased version stating: the `LitT` chunk form changed to the TEAR-RULED CANDIDATE C zero-prefix uncompressed form (named as such), emitted `.beam` bytes differ from 0.15.2's output for any module with literals, downstream content hashes over emitted bytes will shift once, and the decode side is unchanged and loads both forms. The build handoff carries the same statement plus the claim-scope line (cross-consumer determinism, aion version identity). Version-number roll itself stays with the release process (`scripts/release.sh`, project-lead gated) and is out of scope here.
acceptance: CHANGELOG.md carries the identity-shift entry with the ruled form named and the one-time hash-shift stated as expected behaviour
The handoff records the claim scope and the hash-shift statement
No version-number change is made in this brief

boundaries: CLAIM CARVE-OUT, recorded verbatim from the claim-holder (Vesper Lynd via Waffles, 2026-07-15): (1) NO COLLISION — nothing in-flight on the aion/AWL side edits literals.rs or changes encode output bytes; loader/encode is consumed as an API only. (2) CARVE-OUT CONSENTED as scoped: the compression call plus the golden fixture, NOTHING ELSE in `crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/**` is touchable under this brief, and Vesper Lynd is MANDATORY REVIEWER on the final diff. (3) CLAIM SCOPE: cross-consumer determinism — aion hashes emitted BEAM bytes into durable package version identity, so lockfile-only protection does not protect the actual consumer.

Decode side untouched: `decode_literal_chunk` continues to accept BOTH the zero-prefix uncompressed form and the bounded-inflate compressed form (`crates/beamr/src/loader/decode/chunks.rs:307-313`) — legacy `.beam` files must keep loading forever; flate2 remains a dependency for decode regardless of the ruled candidate.
No new configuration surface, no environment consultation, no silent fallback: the emitted bytes are a pure function of the literal table, or the encoder refuses loudly.
No dependency additions except candidate A's `=`-pinned miniz_oxide, if A is ruled.

Laws stated where they bind: believed-state-is-not-citable-state — every current-state claim above is pinned to file:line at `d60f826`, re-verified at the bytes, never to a research artifact or board message; the measured corpus numbers are reproducible from `crates/beamr/tests/fixtures/**.beam` with a container parse.
Untouchable beyond the carve-out: the rest of `crates/beamr/src/loader/encode/**` and branches `awl-bc1-*` (Vesper's claim).
verification: Run the house bar on the final head: `cargo fmt --all --check`; touch crate roots, then `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` and fresh `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`; `cargo test -p beamr --lib` (default and `--all-features`; telemetry::lifecycle has a known parallel-run flake — rerun standalone and report both honestly if it fires); `cargo test --workspace --all-features --test '*'`; the edge-worker npm leg; both locked wasm32 check commands and the pinned wasm test command with its exact-name inventory unchanged (this brief adds no wasm-inventory names). Run both golden walls in isolation ten consecutive times. Perform, record, and revert R2's fail-first perturbation. Stamp the handoff with final `git rev-parse HEAD`, `rustc --version --verbose` (first two lines), `cargo --version`, `node --version`, UTC timestamps, exact commands, the ruled candidate and its implementation point, the golden fixture location, the identity-shift statement, and the claim-scope line. ORDERING, tear-stated: Vesper Lynd's mandatory review on the final diff happens BEFORE the reviewer-of-record's landing gates, not after; the identity-shift statement and claim-scope line ride the handoff exactly as R3 writes them.
